text: The robotic instrument elevates the kidney, allowing the fourth arm prograsp instrument to remove the robotic bulldog clamps from the renal hilum. The prograsp instrument grasps the notch of the clamps, enabling precise control during removal. The clamp on the renal vein is removed first, followed by the clamp on the renal artery.

After unclamping the renal hilum, the surgeon places additional sutures to close the renal defect. Hemlock clips are cinched to tighten the closure, and laparotide clips are used to secure them in place. If bleeding is observed, further sutures and hemlock clips are applied to control it.
